prevalence.value: ~2-5% (children; higher in tropical and crowded settings)
prevalence.source: Tropical dermatology surveillance; pediatric primary care syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; outbreaks in crowded schools and slums

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; high morbidity and secondary infection risk
burden.source: WHO skin and soft tissue infection context

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis of honey-colored crusted lesions; culture if MRSA suspected or recurrent outbreaks

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with prompt antibiotics and hygiene measures

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis common; culture testing limited to tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Topical mupirocin for limited lesions; oral antibiotics for extensive disease; hygiene measures
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Over-the-counter antibiotic use and resistance concerns
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost antibiotics and primary care access for early treatment
